question_text: A light bulb converts 40 J of electrical energy into 10 J of light energy and 30 J of heat energy. What is the efficiency of the light bulb?
explanation: Efficiency = (useful energy out ÷ total energy in) × 100. The useful energy is the light (10 J) and the total energy input is 40 J. So efficiency = (10 ÷ 40) × 100 = 25%.
